description:
You are a junior log-analyst who has been given a single access log that lives in the user’s home directory:

        /home/user/logs/webserver_access.log

Your manager wants two very small artefacts:

1.  A ranked list of the three IP addresses that made the **largest number of requests on
    15 Feb 2023 only**.
2.  A tiny “run book” that records exactly which terminal commands you issued to obtain
    the result.

Do NOT over-engineer this.  
Stick to a *maximum* of three shell commands (pipes are fine).  
The exercise is about showing that you can perform quick, one-liner style
diagnostics.

Step-by-step goals the automated checker will verify:

A.  Produce the list of the three busiest IPs for **15 Feb 2023** and save it to  
        /home/user/logs/analysis/feb15_top_ips.log  
    The file **must** contain exactly three lines, nothing more, nothing less.  
    Each line must have the format  

            <ip-address><single space><request-count>

    Example of correct formatting (DO NOT hard-code these values):  
            192.0.2.50 17  
            198.51.100.27 13  
            203.0.113.8 9

    •  Lines must be sorted in descending order by the numeric request count  
       (highest count first).  
    •  There must be a single final newline at the end of the file.

B.  Create a second file that records how you obtained the answer:  
        /home/user/logs/analysis/commands_used.log  
    •  Write each shell command you executed, one per line, **in the exact order
       you executed them**.  
    •  The file must contain no more than three lines because you may not use more
       than three commands for this task.  
    •  Do not include prompts (“$ ”) or any extra commentary—only the raw commands.

Directory requirements:

• If /home/user/logs/analysis/ does not exist at the start, create it.  
• All files created must inherit the default user permissions; do not attempt to
  obtain root privileges.

Hints you may choose to follow (not mandatory):

• Common tools for this kind of job are grep, awk, cut, sort, uniq, head.  
• Remember that the log entries are in combined Apache/Nginx format with the
  timestamp enclosed in square brackets; filter on “15/Feb/2023”.  
• You can build everything with one long pipeline or a couple of shorter ones—up
  to you, as long as no more than three distinct commands are used overall.

When you are finished, the two files listed above should exist with the exact
formats described. The automated tests will inspect:

1. That /home/user/logs/analysis/feb15_top_ips.log exists, has exactly three
   lines, the lines match the required “IP COUNT” pattern and are ordered
   correctly.
2. That /home/user/logs/analysis/commands_used.log exists, contains one to three
   non-empty lines, and that each line is a valid shell command.

No additional output is necessary once the files are in place.
truth:
Initial environment:

1. The directory /home/user/logs/ exists and already contains one file named
   webserver_access.log with the following 15 lines (Apache combined format):

203.0.113.1 - - [15/Feb/2023:08:57:03 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
198.51.100.2 - - [15/Feb/2023:09:10:44 +0000] "GET /about HTTP/1.1" 200 850
192.0.2.3 - - [14/Feb/2023:23:59:59 +0000] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 200 600
203.0.113.1 - - [15/Feb/2023:09:12:15 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 302 517
198.51.100.2 - - [15/Feb/2023:09:13:47 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
203.0.113.1 - - [15/Feb/2023:09:14:23 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
203.0.113.1 - - [15/Feb/2023:09:20:55 +0000] "GET /products HTTP/1.1" 200 4096
192.0.2.3 - - [15/Feb/2023:09:22:07 +0000] "GET /contact HTTP/1.1" 404 256
198.51.100.2 - - [15/Feb/2023:09:30:18 +0000] "GET /pricing HTTP/1.1" 200 1536
203.0.113.5 - - [16/Feb/2023:07:00:01 +0000] "GET /old-page HTTP/1.1" 301 245
203.0.113.1 - - [15/Feb/2023:10:01:33 +0000] "GET /blog HTTP/1.1" 200 11200
198.51.100.9 - - [15/Feb/2023:11:22:10 +0000] "GET /random HTTP/1.1" 200 100
203.0.113.7 - - [13/Feb/2023:17:45:22 +0000] "GET /archive HTTP/1.1" 200 640
192.0.2.3 - - [15/Feb/2023:12:00:00 +0000] "GET /contact HTTP/1.1" 404 256
203.0.113.1 - - [15/Feb/2023:12:15:45 +0000] "GET /cart HTTP/1.1" 200 768

2. No /home/user/logs/analysis/ directory exists at the start.

Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes the task:

A. /home/user/logs/analysis/feb15_top_ips.log contains exactly:

203.0.113.1 5
198.51.100.2 3
192.0.2.3 2

B. /home/user/logs/analysis/commands_used.log exists and contains
   one to three non-empty lines, each line being a shell command.
   The exact commands may vary, so they are not specified here; the
   automated test will only check the count of lines (≤3) and that each
   is non-blank ASCII text without prompt symbols.

The test suite will also confirm that:

• The feb15_top_ips.log file ends with a single newline and has
  no extra trailing blank lines.
• Both new files are regular files owned by the default user and
  readable by the test harness.
